problem.title: Proposed Problem
paragraphs: How can coconut pests and diseases be classified while providing real-time surveillance to stakeholders?

Weligama Coconut Leaf Wilt Disease (WCLWD) and Coconut Caterpillar Infestation (CCI) are two of the most threatening conditions for coconut cultivation in Sri Lanka. Due to WCLWD, about 300,000 palms were removed and an estimated 60,000 palms required identification for removal. Significant yield loss can occur when CCI causes more than 30% defoliation. Efficient management therefore requires early detection and fast communication between growers and professionals.

solution.title: Proposed Solution
paragraphs: Coco Remedy uses mobile and web-based software to manage WCLWD and CCI, both of which heavily affect coconut cultivation.

For WCLWD, yellowing patterns can overlap with magnesium deficiency. For CCI, dried leaf appearance can be confused with leaf scorching. Deep learning techniques, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), are used for accurate feature extraction and disease identification.

Coconut growers, estate managers, CRISL researchers, CDOs of CCB, and the general public can identify diseased palms by capturing leaf images. Geo-tagged locations can support anonymous data collection and future prediction workflows.

WCLWD severity is estimated from multiple symptoms. For CCI, caterpillar counts are extracted using OpenCV and YOLO-based object detection, while infected regions are estimated through Mask R-CNN. Progression level is then determined from these parameters.

When infected trees are identified, the system sends real-time notifications. Infection records, locations, and severity levels support timely remedial actions, and danger zones can be visualized through mapping technologies so nearby users can take precautions.
